macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Q1 GDP Grows at 2% Annual Rate Amid Slowdown
items.summary: The US economy expanded at a 2% annualized rate in Q1 2026, below the 2.2% forecast but rebounding from prior quarter, driven by AI investment up 8.7% while consumer spending slowed to 1.6%. Inflation pressures from Iran war-related energy spikes cloud the outlook.

items.title: PCE Inflation Rises to 3.5% YoY, Exceeding Fed Target
items.summary: March PCE price index surged 3.5% year-over-year, up from 2.8% in February, fueled by higher oil and gas prices from the Iran war; Fed holds rates at 3.5%-3.75% amid inflation concerns despite strong jobs data.

items.title: Weekly Jobless Claims Hit Lowest Level Since 1969
items.summary: Initial jobless claims came in at record lows, signaling a low-hire, low-fire labor market with unemployment remaining historically low, providing positive offset to inflation and growth slowdown.

items.title: Iran War Drives Gasoline to $4.30/Gallon, Brent Crude Over $126
items.summary: Energy prices skyrocketed due to Iran conflict disrupting Strait of Hormuz oil flows, pushing US gasoline to $4.30 per gallon—highest since 2022—and Brent crude to wartime highs above $126/barrel.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Dogecoin Leads Meme Sector Surge with 7.40% Gain
items.summary: The Meme sector rose 3.89% in the past 24 hours, driven by Dogecoin's 7.40% increase. Other gainers include Aptos up 4.84% and Rain up 4.56%.

items.title: $492 Million in Crypto Liquidations Amid Market Bloodbath
items.summary: The cryptocurrency market saw $492 million in liquidations over the last 24 hours, primarily affecting long positions. This reflects heightened volatility following recent price dips.

items.title: Over 500 Ethereum Wallets Drained in Major Exploit
items.summary: Attackers drained more than 500 dormant Ethereum wallets, some inactive for up to seven years, causing up to $800K in losses. Funds were swapped via ThorChain and bridged to Bitcoin network.

items.title: Bitcoin Holds Near $76K Despite 3% Dip and Record Inflows
items.summary: Bitcoin traded near $76K after a 3% decline from $79K, supported by record nine-day inflows of $2.12 billion. Ethereum fell 2.21% while Dogecoin gained 6.19%.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Google Signs Classified AI Deal with Pentagon
items.summary: Google has joined OpenAI and xAI as a U.S. Department of Defense contractor, agreeing to supply AI models for classified use including mission planning and weapons targeting. The agreement requires Google to adjust its AI safety settings and filters.
items.source: MIT Sloan Management Review - AI Dispatch

items.title: OpenAI Plans AI-Centric Smartphone Launch by 2028
items.summary: OpenAI is reportedly developing next-generation smartphone processors in partnership with Qualcomm and MediaTek, with mass production anticipated by 2028 to rival Apple's iPhone.
items.source: MIT Sloan Management Review - AI Dispatch

items.title: Microsoft and OpenAI Revise Partnership Agreement
items.summary: Microsoft and OpenAI announced amendments to their partnership that end Microsoft's revenue-share payments, strip cloud exclusivity, and convert Microsoft's intellectual property license to a non-exclusive arrangement.
items.source: MIT Sloan Management Review - AI Dispatch

items.title: Big Tech's AI Infrastructure Spending Reaches $700 Billion Annually
items.summary: Combined capital expenditures from Alphabet, Amazon, Meta, and Microsoft exceeded $130 billion in the latest quarter, with annual AI spending projected to surpass $700 billion in 2026, up from $410 billion last year.

items.title: SoftBank Plans $100 Billion AI and Robotics IPO
items.summary: SoftBank Group is preparing to spin out and take public a new AI and robotics company called 'Roze,' targeting a valuation of up to $100 billion in what would be one of the largest AI IPOs to date.

items.title: UAE Plans to Run 50% of Government on Agentic AI Within Two Years
items.summary: UAE Prime Minister Sheikh Mohammed bin Rashid Al Maktoum announced a directive for the country to have 50% of federal government operations running on agentic AI within two years.
items.source: MIT Sloan Management Review - AI Dispatch

items.title: OpenAI's o1 Model Shows Promise in Medical Diagnosis
items.summary: Research testing OpenAI's advanced o1 language model found it could accurately diagnose patients with various medical conditions, demonstrating AI's potential to match or exceed doctor performance in diagnostic tasks.

items.title: LLMs Help Scientists Identify Unexplored Research Topics
items.summary: Researchers at Germany's Karlsruhe Institute of Technology used the LLaMa-2-13B model to analyze materials science publications and generate predictions for future research areas more precisely than traditional algorithms.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: TMNT VR Game Launches for Quest and PC
items.summary: The first Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles VR game, built from the ground up for VR, has released today for Quest and PC platforms.

items.title: 007: First Light James Bond Game Previewed
items.summary: New gameplay footage and impressions of 007: First Light, a upcoming action-adventure James Bond game, suggest it could be the best in the series.

items.title: Where Winds Meet Releases Patch Notes and Events
items.summary: Latest patch notes and events for Where Winds Meet have been announced, including tips on gear materials and new content.

items.title: Diablo Immortal Introduces Auto Battle Terror Rifts Meta
items.summary: New auto battle strategies using Terror Rifts are the top meta for efficient farming and rewards in Diablo Immortal following recent updates.

items.title: Guild Wars 2 Windrose Update Review After 34 Hours
items.summary: Early review of Guild Wars 2's Windrose update praises ship combat similarities to Assassin's Creed Black Flag and sea shanties.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Apple Reports Q2 Revenue Up 17% to $111.18B, Beats Estimates with Record iPhone and Services Revenue
items.summary: Apple announced Q2 earnings with revenue of $111.18 billion, up 17% year-over-year and surpassing $109.66 billion estimates, alongside net income up 19% to $29.6 billion and a new $100 billion share buyback authorization. Tim Cook noted iPhone sales were constrained by chip supply despite strong demand.

items.title: Atlassian Q3 Revenue Surges 32% to $1.79B, Raises Annual Forecast as Stock Jumps 17%
items.summary: Atlassian reported third-quarter revenue of $1.79 billion, exceeding $1.69 billion estimates and up 32% year-over-year, prompting an upward revision to its full-year revenue guidance. The company's stock rose over 17% in after-hours trading.
